assistantText: The old process is still alive — SIGTERM didn't take it down. It's been running since June 14 with 22 threads, possibly stuck in a blocking call. Let me force-kill it.

toolCalls.name: execute_code
toolCalls.preview: 🐍 import os, signal, subprocess, time # Force kill os.kill(7204, signal.SIGKILL) print("SIGKILL sent to PID 7204") time.sleep(2) # Verify it's gone try: os.kill(7204, 0) print("Process still alive?!") except ProcessLookupError: print("Process is dead. Good.") # Wait for s6 to restart time.sleep(3) # Check for new gateway process result = subprocess.run(["pgrep", "-f", "hermes gateway run"], capture_output=True, text=True) pids = result.stdout.strip().split() print(f"New gateway PIDs: {pids}") if pids: for pid in pids: r = subprocess.run(["ps", "-p", pid, "-o", "pid,etime,args"], capture_output=True, text=True) print(f" {r.stdout.strip()}") # Check gateway log for recent activity for log in ["/opt/data/.hermes/gateway.log", "/tmp/hermes-gateway.log"]: if os.path.exists(log): r = subprocess.run(["tail", "-10", log], capture_output=True, text=True) if r.stdout.strip(): print(f"\n--- Last lines of {log} ---") print(r.stdout.strip())
